title: Don’t Be Triggered — Qur’an, emotions, and self-mastery
summary: Al-Baqarah 2:216 retrains likes and dislikes; a parable of the hakim shows how words hijack the heart, contrasted with detachment, the Qur’an as an “operating manual,” and adab of the servants of the Merciful—patience when others are a trial.

title: School of Eternity — tribulation, patience, and the believer
summary: Sacred “text messages” versus endless news; Baqarah 155–157 as divine forewarning; Kierkegaard’s school of eternity meets patience in obedience; the hadith of the believer; Zarruq and Imam al-Junayd on imperfection, dunya, and contentment.

title: Mastering your attention over distraction — The Game of mind
summary: What you give your attention to shapes your reality; Islamic tradition links distraction (lahw) to a scattered heart, and attention to Allah to waking up before death wakes you.

title: When Your Heart is On Empty
summary: A “heart like stone” can name steadfastness under pain, not only hardness; the Qur’an and the story of Ya'qub frame grief, numbness, and keeping revelation and empathy alive through action when emotion thins.

title: How To Diagnose Your Heart
summary: Four types of hearts in hadith; a healthy pulse includes regret after sin and pleasure in worship, one master for the heart, and the Qur’an as daily exercise—not emotional performance alone.
